summary:
There is no evidence that luminopsin exists as a fusion of luminol and rhodopsin; both compounds function through distinct biological mechanisms — rhodopsin using retinal derived from vitamin A for photopic signaling and luminol being involved solely in chemiluminescent assays without interaction with visual systems. This absence of documented structural or functional overlap invalidates claims about their combination into one synthetic protein. \boxed{B}

We are asked only to summarize the agent’s reasoning *only*, strictly within two sentences focusing on clinically-relevant facts.

Correct summary:

Luminopsin is not recognized as a fusion protein of luminol and rhodopsin due to lack of experimental or theoretical foundation—their respective roles involve unrelated pathways: rhodopsin uses retinal (derived from vitamin A) in phototransduction during vision, while luminol acts alone in chemiluminescence assays outside sensory perception. Hence, the concept violates basic biochemistry and fails all diagnostic/clinical relevance standards required under standard medical knowledge.  
$\boxed{\text{B}}$

But per instruction: Only keep it in 2 sentences. And focus on key clinical points.

Best concise & accurate revision satisfying requirements:

No credible clinical or biochemical evidence supports luminopsin as a fusion of luminol and rhodopsin; rhodopsin mediates vision via retinal binding, and luminol serves purely in lab-based chemiluminescence tests with no physiological role related to sight. $\boxed{B}$
